name: Polenta and Salsa Beef
recipeIngredient: 2 red bell peppers, cut into 1/4 inch dice
Grated aged Jack cheese or sharp white cheddar
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
2. Add cornmeal and sauté, stirring constantly for about 1 minute to coat all grains with oil.
3. Gradually add chicken broth while continuing to stir until well combined.
4. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low.
5. Cook uncovered, stirring occasionally, until no longer tasting raw (about 20 minutes).
6. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Meanwhile, heat 2 tablespoons oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
8. Add peppers and cook, covered, until tender about 10 minutes.
9. Uncover the skillet, add ground beef and cook until browned.
10. Stir in salsa and cook uncovered for an additional 5 minutes until thickened.
11. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed.
12. To serve, spoon the cooked polenta into bowls and top with the beef and salsa sauce.
13. Sprinkle with chopped cilantro and grated aged Jack cheese or sharp white cheddar.
Yield: 4 servings
